title: Valley of Flowers National Park
tagline: Valley of Flowers National Park — wildlife reserve of Uttarakhand

heroImage.alt: Valley of Flowers National Park, Uttarakhand

overview.short: Valley of Flowers National Park is an Indian national park which was established in 1982.
overview.description: Valley of Flowers National Park is an Indian national park which was established in 1982. It is located in Chamoli in the state of Uttarakhand and is known for its meadows of endemic alpine flowers and the variety of flora. This richly diverse area is also home to rare and endangered animals, including the Asiatic black bear, snow leopard, musk deer, brown bear, red fox and blue sheep. Birds found in the park include Himalayan monal pheasant and other high-altitude birds.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Hemkund Sahib
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Hemkund Sahib is a Sikh place of worship (gurdwara) and pilgrimage site in Chamoli district, Uttarakhand, India. It is devoted to Guru Gobind Singh (1666–1708), the tenth Sikh Guru, and finds mention in Dasam Granth.

topPlaces.name: Ghangaria
topPlaces.description: Ghangaria is a village in Uttarakhand state of India. It is located at an altitude of 3049 meters.

topPlaces.name: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks
topPlaces.description: The Nanda Devi National Park and Valley of Flowers National Parks is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Uttarakhand, India. It possesses of two core areas about 20 km apart, made up by the Nanda Devi National Park and the Valley of Flowers National Park, plus an encompassing Combined Buffer Zone.

image.alt: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks

topPlaces.name: Nilgiri Parbat
topPlaces.description: Nilgiri Parbat is a mountain of the Garhwal Himalaya in Uttarakhand India and rises to an elevation of 6,474 metres (21,240 ft) with a prominence is 893 metres (2,930 ft). It is 91st highest located entirely within Uttarakhand.

topPlaces.name: Nar Parvat
topPlaces.description: Nar Parvat is a mountain peak located at 5,855 m (19,209 ft) above sea level in the state of Uttarakhand in India and the prominence is 871 m (2,858 ft).

topPlaces.name: Gauri Parbat
topPlaces.description: Gauri Parbat, also spelled Ghori Parbat is a mountain peak, located in Uttarakhand state, India and the height of the peak is 6,708 meters (22,008 feet). Also called "Horse" peak, it is near Joshimath and another peak nearby is Hathi parbat 6727 metres (22070 feet).

topPlaces.name: Hathi Parbat
topPlaces.description: Hathi Parbat, also known as Elephant Peak, is a mountain in the Garhwal Himalayas in India. It is located in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand state.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valley of Flowers National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valley of Flowers National Park is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Valley of Flowers National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (150 km) and the nearest railway station is Dehradun Railway Station (159 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valley of Flowers National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valley of Flowers National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valley of Flowers National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Valley of Flowers National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valley of Flowers National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gurdwara Hemkund Sahib, Ghangaria, Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks, Nilgiri Parbat, Nar Parvat.
